comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Design doc written: docs/adr/0007-editing-prompt-contracts-from-the-editor.md + companion pointer in docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md §1.4. Decision: (1) prompt inputs/outputs schema NAMES editable via the existing 6592 prompt-save form with a registry pick-list + fail-closed write-time resolve-check; reuse (not re-solve) the existing prompt_override_drift gate by factoring its outputs-diff into a shared helper the editor calls at write time (block-then-confirm on breaking outputs-override); no schema_version bump / no new front-matter key. (2) scripted-op @register_step contracts NOT editable — code is the single source (no file artifact; import-time STEP_CONTRACTS; a runtime override would re-create the ADR-0006 view-vs-runtime divergence). Inline schemas explicitly out of scope. 6 follow-on stories identified. make lint green.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Editing a prompt's TEXT from the visual editor is ALREADY DELIVERED (story 6592 — "Editor prompt library + authoring/write-back", closed under epic da27): the prompt library can create + edit prompt text in place with atomic write-back and derived-index regen. What is still surfaced READ-ONLY and has NO recorded design is editing a step's CONTRACT (a prompt's front-matter `inputs`/`outputs` schema NAMES, and the scripted-op contract that lives in code). This ticket is now narrowed to BRAINSTORM/DESIGN the contract-editing question only.

## What (to explore — not yet decided)
- Editing a PROMPT's contract (front-matter `inputs`/`outputs` schema names) from the editor: how to pick/validate a registry schema; what happens to overrides (the override-drift gate); breaking-change UX for existing consumers.
- The scripted-op (Python `@register_step`) contract boundary: can/should op contracts ever be editable from the editor? (almost certainly NO — code is the source) — document the boundary explicitly and why.
- Provenance + safety: content hashes, readers-before-writers `schema_version` policy, refusing edits that would fork a shared prompt or silently break consumers.

## Scope
In: a short design doc / ADR (options + tradeoffs + recommendation) for editing prompt CONTRACTS from the editor, plus the explicit op-contract-in-code boundary decision. Out: prompt-text editing (done via 6592); implementation (its own follow-on stories); changing the op-contract-in-code model.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A short design doc / ADR captures the contract-editing options above with tradeoffs and a recommended approach.
- [ ] The decision states explicitly whether scripted-op contracts are editable from the editor (and why), and how prompt-contract editing preserves single-source-of-truth, the derived index, content-hash provenance, and the override-drift gate.
- [ ] Notes prompt-TEXT editing as already delivered (story 6592); follow-on implementation stories are identified from the chosen approach.

file_impact.reason: the design doc / ADR this ticket delivers
brief companion pointer to the new ADR

compiled_state.title: Brainstorm: allow editing a prompt CONTRACT from the visual editor (prompt-text editing shipped via 6592)
